trades.sentiment_summary: Credo Technology Group Holding Ltd (CRDO) shows strong positive sentiment with institutional buying by Citigroup of 349,209 shares, robust analyst consensus of Buy ratings, and AI-driven trading signals indicating overweight bias across time horizons[1][3][4]. The stock traded up 2.15% to $130.66, supported by recent strong earnings beats and revenue growth of 272.1% year-over-year[3][6]. Note that negative news pertains to unrelated Credo Brands Marketing Ltd hitting an all-time low[2].

Sprouts Farmers Market (SFM) stock rose nearly 6% on Monday to close at $71.50 and held near $72 in Tuesday premarket, driven by a $1 billion share buyback announcement and plans for 40+ new stores despite guidance for flat to slightly negative comparable sales in 2026[1][3][5]. Management acknowledged challenges in early 2026, including weaker customer traffic, prompting leadership changes in merchandising and customer divisions[1][4]. Analyst consensus remains 'Moderate Buy' or 'Buy' with a $108.15 target price, supported by recent institutional investment[2][3].

ADMA Biologics announced it will report Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results on February 25, 2026, after market close, followed by a conference call at 4:30 p.m. ET.[1][4] Shares closed up 2.06% at $16.32, outperforming the healthcare sector amid rising institutional interest ahead of the earnings release.[2] Recent price action shows mild gains of 0.25% to 0.59% on announcement days, building on strong prior quarters with revenue growth and raised guidance.[1][3]
